questions.question: What is the primary difference between haloalkanes and haloarenes?
options: Haloalkanes have halogen attached to sp2 carbon; haloarenes to sp3
Haloalkanes have halogen attached to sp3 carbon; haloarenes to sp2
Haloalkanes are always gases; haloarenes are always liquids
questions.explanation: Haloalkanes contain halogen atoms attached to the sp3 hybridised carbon of an alkyl group, whereas haloarenes contain halogen attached to the sp2 hybridised carbon of an aryl group.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which halogen-containing antibiotic is used for the treatment of typhoid fever?
questions.explanation: Chloramphenicol, produced by soil microorganisms, is very effective for the treatment of typhoid fever.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which compound is used as an anaesthetic during surgery?
questions.explanation: Halothane is used as an anaesthetic during surgery.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Compounds containing two halogen atoms on the same carbon atom are called:
questions.explanation: Dihaloalkanes having the same type of halogen atoms are named as alkylidene halides or gem-dihalides when both halogens are on the same carbon.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: In the nomenclature of dihalides, 'vic-dihalides' refer to:
questions.explanation: Vicinal dihalides (or vic-dihalides) have halogen atoms on adjacent carbon atoms.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: What is the common name for the compound CH2=CH-CH2X?
questions.explanation: Allylic halides are compounds in which the halogen atom is bonded to an sp3 hybridised carbon atom adjacent to a carbon-carbon double bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which of the following is the best reagent to prepare alkyl chlorides from alcohols to ensure high purity?
questions.explanation: Thionyl chloride is preferred because the other two products (SO2 and HCl) are escapable gases, leaving behind pure alkyl halides.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Free radical chlorination of butane (CH3CH2CH2CH3) yields:
questions.explanation: Free radical chlorination usually gives a mixture of isomeric mono- and polyhaloalkanes which are difficult to separate.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: According to Markovnikov's rule, when HI adds to propene, the major product is:
questions.explanation: Markovnikov's rule states that the negative part of the addendum adds to the carbon with fewer hydrogen atoms. For propene, this yields 2-iodopropane.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which reaction is used for the synthesis of alkyl fluorides?
questions.explanation: The synthesis of alkyl fluorides is best accomplished by heating an alkyl chloride/bromide in the presence of a metallic fluoride (AgF, Hg2F2, etc.), known as the Swarts reaction.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The Finkelstein reaction is used specifically for the preparation of alkyl ________.
questions.explanation: Alkyl iodides are often prepared by the reaction of alkyl chlorides/bromides with NaI in dry acetone.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The conversion of primary aromatic amines into haloarenes using sodium nitrite, HCl, and cuprous salts is known as:
questions.explanation: Sandmeyer’s reaction involves replacing the diazonium group with Cl or Br using cuprous salts.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Why do haloalkanes have higher boiling points than the parent hydrocarbons of comparable molecular mass?
options: Greater polarity and higher molecular mass leading to stronger intermolecular forces
questions.explanation: Intermolecular forces (dipole-dipole and van der Waals) are stronger in the halogen derivatives.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: For a given alkyl group, what is the order of boiling points of alkyl halides?
questions.explanation: Boiling point increases with the size and mass of the halogen atom due to increased van der Waals forces.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: What happens to the boiling point of isomeric haloalkanes as branching increases?
questions.explanation: Boiling points of isomeric haloalkanes decrease with increase in branching because the surface area decreases, weakening van der Waals forces.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Haloalkanes are only very slightly soluble in water because:
options: They cannot form hydrogen bonds with water
The energy released during new attractions is less than required to break existing bonds
questions.explanation: Energy is required to overcome attractions between haloalkane molecules and break hydrogen bonds between water molecules. The energy released when new bonds form is not sufficient.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The SN2 reaction mechanism is characterized by:
questions.explanation: In SN2, the nucleophile attacks from the opposite side, leading to the inversion of configuration.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The order of reactivity of alkyl halides towards SN2 reaction is:
questions.explanation: SN2 reactivity depends on steric hindrance; primary halides are least hindered and most reactive.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The SN1 reaction follows which order of kinetics?
questions.explanation: SN1 reactions (substitution nucleophilic unimolecular) follow first order kinetics as the rate depends only on the concentration of the alkyl halide.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The order of reactivity of alkyl halides towards SN1 reaction is:
questions.explanation: SN1 reactivity depends on the stability of the carbocation formed; tertiary carbocations are the most stable.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: What is an 'ambident' nucleophile?
options: A nucleophile with two nucleophilic centers
A nucleophile that acts as an acid
questions.explanation: Groups like cyanides and nitrites possess two nucleophilic centers and are called ambident nucleophiles.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: When alkyl halides react with AgCN, the major product is:
questions.explanation: AgCN is covalent; the nitrogen atom is free to donate electrons, leading to the formation of isocyanides as the major product.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which of the following describes 'Chirality'?
options: Objects that are superimposable on their mirror image
Objects that are non-superimposable on their mirror image
questions.explanation: The objects which are non-superimposable on their mirror image are said to be chiral and this property is known as chirality.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: A mixture containing two enantiomers in equal proportions will have:
questions.explanation: A mixture of equal parts enantiomers will have zero optical rotation as the rotation of one isomer is cancelled by the other. This is a racemic mixture.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: According to Zaitsev rule, in a dehydrohalogenation reaction, the preferred product is the alkene which has:
options: The least number of alkyl groups
The greatest number of alkyl groups attached to the doubly bonded carbon atoms
questions.explanation: Zaitsev rule states that the more highly substituted alkene is the preferred product in elimination reactions.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Grignard reagents are formed by the reaction of alkyl halides with ________ in dry ether.
questions.explanation: Alkyl magnesium halides (RMgX) are called Grignard reagents, prepared by reacting RX with Mg in dry ether.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Why must Grignard reagents be prepared under anhydrous conditions?
options: They are highly reactive and react with any source of proton (like water) to give hydrocarbons
questions.explanation: Grignard reagents are very reactive and will react with moisture to form alkanes.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The Wurtz reaction is used to prepare ________ from alkyl halides using sodium metal.
options: Hydrocarbons containing double the number of carbon atoms
questions.explanation: Wurtz reaction involves the coupling of two alkyl groups using sodium in dry ether to form a higher alkane.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Aryl halides are extremely less reactive towards nucleophilic substitution compared to alkyl halides because of:
options: Difference in hybridisation of carbon (sp2 vs sp3)
questions.explanation: Resonance, sp2 hybridisation of C-X bond, and potential repulsion are all reasons for the low reactivity of aryl halides.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The presence of an electron-withdrawing group (like -NO2) at which position(s) increases the reactivity of haloarenes?
questions.explanation: Electron-withdrawing groups at ortho and para positions facilitate nucleophilic attack by stabilising the intermediate carbanion.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The reaction where two molecules of aryl halides join in the presence of sodium to give diphenyl is called:
questions.explanation: Fittig reaction involves the coupling of two aryl groups.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which of the following is known as Freon-12?
questions.explanation: Dichlorodifluoromethane (CCl2F2) is known as Freon-12.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: What is the full form of DDT?
questions.explanation: DDT stands for p,p'-Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which polyhalogen compound was used widely as an insecticide but later banned due to environmental persistence?
questions.explanation: DDT was the first chlorinated organic insecticide, banned in many countries due to its stability and harm to wildlife.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which property of Iodoform makes it useful as an antiseptic?
questions.explanation: The antiseptic property of iodoform is due to the liberation of free iodine, not due to iodoform itself.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: In the naming of haloarenes, the prefix 'p' stands for:
questions.explanation: In common names of disubstituted benzenes, 'o' stands for ortho (1,2), 'm' for meta (1,3), and 'p' for para (1,4).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: What is the IUPAC name for isopropyl chloride?
questions.explanation: Isopropyl chloride has the chlorine on the second carbon of a three-carbon chain.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Chloroform is stored in closed dark coloured bottles completely filled so that:
options: Air is kept out to prevent oxidation to poisonous phosgene
Light doesn't reach it to prevent reduction
It doesn't react with the bottle
questions.explanation: Chloroform is slowly oxidised by air in the presence of light to an extremely poisonous gas, carbonyl chloride, also known as phosgene.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Exposure to carbon tetrachloride (CCl4) can cause:
questions.explanation: CCl4 has severe health effects including nerve damage and liver cancer, and environmental effects like ozone depletion.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The dipole moment of chlorobenzene is ________ than that of cyclohexyl chloride.
questions.explanation: Due to sp2 hybridisation and resonance, the C-Cl bond in chlorobenzene has less ionic character and is shorter, resulting in a lower dipole moment.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a vinylic halide?
questions.explanation: Vinylic halides are compounds in which the halogen is bonded to an sp2 hybridised carbon of a carbon-carbon double bond.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The C-X bond length ________ from fluorine to iodine.
questions.explanation: As the size of the halogen atom increases down the group, the C-X bond length increases.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Alkyl iodides darken on standing because:
questions.explanation: Alkyl iodides and bromides develop color when exposed to light due to decomposition.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: SN2 reactions are favored in ________ solvents.
questions.explanation: SN2 reactions are generally favored by polar aprotic solvents that don't solvate the nucleophile strongly.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Reaction of alkyl halide with aqueous KOH yields:
questions.explanation: Nucleophilic substitution by hydroxide ion (OH-) converts alkyl halides to alcohols.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: The spatial arrangement of four different groups around a central carbon is called ________ geometry.
questions.explanation: sp3 hybridised carbon with four different substituents forms a tetrahedral geometry.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Enantiomers have identical physical properties EXCEPT for:
options: Direction of rotation of plane polarised light
questions.explanation: Enantiomers differ only in their interaction with plane polarised light and other chiral molecules.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: Friedel-Crafts alkylation of chlorobenzene yields ________ as the major product.
questions.explanation: The chlorine atom is ortho and para directing; the para isomer is usually the major product due to less steric hindrance.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: What is the product of the reaction between chlorobenzene and acetyl chloride in the presence of AlCl3?
questions.explanation: This is a Friedel-Crafts acylation reaction yielding ortho and para-substituted acetophenone.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.question: A compound with the formula C6H6Cl6 is known as:
questions.explanation: Benzene hexachloride (BHC), also called Gammaxene or Lindane, has the formula C6H6Cl6.
questions.subject: Chemistry
